Sherman Robinson and wife, Flora Robinson
document_extractions.legal_description_full:
Deed 1 (420 Watson Street): Being known as 420 Watson Street and more particularly described as follows: Beginning at a point on the west side of Watson Street 145 feet north of its intersection with Cabarrus Street, being the northeast corner of a lot formerly owned by Jennie Mayes and the southeast corner of the lot herein conveyed; runs west along the north line of formerly Mayes' lot 90 feet; thence north in a line parallel with Watson Street 45 feet to the south line of Lot No. 4 as shown on the plat of the Watson Lands, recorded in Book of Maps 1885, at page 25, in the office of the Register of Deeds for Wake County, North Carolina; thence east along the south line of Lot No. 4, 90 feet to the west side of Watson Street; thence south along the west side of Watson Street 45 feet to the point of beginning, and being Lot No. 5 as shown on the plat of the Watson Lands recorded in Book of Maps 1885, page 25, in the office of the Register of Deeds for Wake County, North Carolina, and being premises known as No. 420 Watson Street, Raleigh, North Carolina. For further reference see Book 595, page 402, Wake County Registry. The above property is conveyed subject to the pro rata part of the 1946 City and County Taxes.

Deed 2 (530 Bragg Street): Being known as 530 Bragg Street and more particularly described as follows: Beginning at a point in the southern line of Bragg Street, said point being 100 feet east of the intersection of Mineral Springs Branch and the southern line of Bragg Street runs thence in a southern direction 205 feet to a point in Mineral Springs Branch; thence in an eastern direction with said branch 51 feet; thence in a northern direction 217.1 feet to a point in the southern line of Bragg Street; thence in a western direction along the southern line of Bragg Street 50 feet to the point of beginning, being Lot No. 3 according to map registered in the office of the Register of Deeds for Wake County, North Carolina, in Book of Maps 1928, on page 115. The above property is conveyed subject to the prorata part of the 1946 County taxes.
document_extractions.legal_description_short: Lot No. 5, Watson Lands plat (Book of Maps 1885, page 25), known as 420 Watson Street, Raleigh, NC; and Lot No. 3 per Book of Maps 1928, page 115, known as 530 Bragg Street, Raleigh, NC
document_extractions.references_prior_deed_language: For further reference see Book 595, page 402, Wake County Registry.
document_extractions.legal_match_notes: No parcel_reference short legal, subdivision, lot, or plat data was provided to match against. The document conveys two distinct parcels (420 Watson Street / Lot 5 Watson Lands, and 530 Bragg Street / Lot 3 Book of Maps 1928 p.115) to two different grantee couples. Without a parcel ID cross-reference or address match, no legal match can be confirmed.
document_extractions.summary: Warranty deed dated May 1, 1946, from Clem B. Holding and wife Katie Lee Holding (joined by I. O. Brady, Trustee, and Durham Life Insurance Company for release of a prior deed of trust) conveying two separate parcels: 420 Watson Street (Lot 5, Watson Lands, Book of Maps 1885 p.25) to Marion Weldon and wife Cora Weldon, and 530 Bragg Street (Lot 3, Book of Maps 1928 p.115) to Sherman Robinson and wife Flora Robinson; recorded in Book 942, Page 81, Wake County Registry on May 25, 1946.

grantee_names: Lydia Hayes, her heirs and assigns
document_extractions.vesting_language: to her only use and behoof forever
document_extractions.legal_description_full: BEGINNING at a stake on the North side of Fort Street (now called Nelson Street), now or formerly Dennis Kearney's corner, and running along said Street in an Easterly direction, 60 feet; thence in a Northerly direction, along line of lands of J. R. Mangum, formerly, now Viola Hicks, 120 feet and 3 inches; thence in a Westerly direction along line of land of Will Debnam, 60 feet to a stake, in Dennis Kearney's line; thence, in a Southerly direction, along line of the Kearney land, 120 feet 3 inches, to the point of BEGINNING.
document_extractions.legal_description_short: Lot on North side of Fort Street (now Nelson Street), Town of Wake Forest, Wake County, NC; 60 ft x 120 ft 3 in, bounded by Kearney, Mangum/Hicks, and Debnam lines.
document_extractions.references_prior_deed_language: on February 3, 1941, W. W. Holding and wife, Josephine H. Holding, executed and delivered a deed to Viola Hicks, conveying a certain lot or lots of land situated in the Town of Wake Forest, Wake County and State of North Carolina, which said deed was duly recorded in the Office of Register of Deeds for Wake County, North Carolina, in Book 860, at page 53
document_extractions.legal_match_notes: No parcel ID, subdivision, lot, or plat reference provided in parcel_reference to match against. Legal description is metes-and-bounds for a lot in Town of Wake Forest, Wake County, NC. Cannot confirm match to parcel 0029590 without additional reference data.
document_extractions.summary: Corrective/quitclaim deed dated June 15, 1954, from Viola Hicks (widow) to Lydia Hayes, conveying a lot on the north side of Fort Street (now Nelson Street) in the Town of Wake Forest, Wake County, NC; recorded June 17, 1954 at Book 1155, Page 171. The deed recites that the lot was inadvertently included in a prior 1941 deed (Book 860, Page 53) from W.W. Holding and wife to Viola Hicks, and was actually intended to be conveyed to Lydia Hayes by a 1944 deed recorded at Book 913, Page 354.

grantor_names: Kathryn Baker Cummings and husband Paul Cummings
W. W. Holding and wife, Josephine H. Holding
grantee_names: D. A. Baker and wife, Annie B. Baker
document_extractions.vesting_language: D. A. Baker and wife, Annie B. Baker their heirs and assigns; Lydia Hayes her heirs and assigns
document_extractions.legal_description_full: 1st. Tract: BEGINNING at the rock ford (White Oak Creek) in C. J. Burt's corner and runs thence up the various courses of said creek to a stake and pointers, corner of lot No. 4; thence 3/4 deg. East 9 chains to a stake and pointers in the line of lot No. 4 corner of lot No. 5; thence North 89 deg. West 16 chains to a stake and pointers in said Burt line; corner of lot No. 5; thence South 5½ deg. West 17.20 chains to the point of beginning, containing 25.17 acres, more or less, and being the same land which was conveyed to Rudolph J. Baker by J. E. Pearson, Commissioner by deed dated October 11, 1930 and recorded in Book 608 at page 217, Wake County Registry. See Book 857 page 449, Wake County Registry, said property herein being lot or tract No. TWO of said deed. 2nd. tract: That tract or parcel of land situated in Buckhorn Township, Wake County, North Carolina, same being lot No. 4 in the Division of the lands of Nancy E. Williams, deceased, containing 14.77 acres, more or less. For a more complete description of said land reference is hereby made to Book 'D' of partitions Page 429, Office of the Register of Deeds for Wake County, North Carolina. Being the same identical land described in a deed dated April 22, 1919, by Clarence J. Smith, Trustee, to J. A. Hockaday, and recorded in Book 338 at page 597, in Wake County Registry. This is the same land that was conveyed to Rudolph J. Baker by J. A. Hockaday and wife Florence Hockaday by deed dated August 20, 1930, and recorded in Book 592 page 426, Wake County Registry. See also Book 857 page 449, Wake County Registry. 3rd. tract: Lying and being in Buckhorn Township, Wake County, and being lot No. 3 as shown on the plat and survey made by C. H. Collins, surveyor, February 1914, attached to the report of Commissioners in Special Proceedings, No. 2031, Record of Partitions 'D' Page 443, in the office of Clerk of the Superior Court, Wake County, and described as follows: BEGINNING at a stake and pointers in F. C. Adams line, corner of lot No. 2 running thence South 3/4 deg. West 2.68 chains to an old field pine, said Adams corner in the line of lot No. 5; thence South 89 deg. East 2.65 chains to a stake and pointers corner of lot No. 5; thence South 3/4 deg. West 8.03 chains to a stake and pointers in the line of lot No. 5 corner of lot No. 4; thence South 87 deg. East 20.55 chains to a stake and pointers just on the West side of the road; thence North 7 deg. East with said road 10.65 chains to a stake and pointers corner of lot No. 2; thence North 87 deg. West 24 chains to the beginning, containing 23.07 acres, more or less, and being the same land allotted to J. C. Williams in the partition of the lands of Nancy Williams by Commissioner C. H. Collins, C. J. Burt and G. H. Holloway in the Special Proceedings before referred to, and being the same land which was conveyed to Rudolph J. Baker by Wm. B. Oliver and wife, see Book 608 page 220, Wake County Registry. [Second deed on same pages:] W. W. Holding and wife, Josephine H. Holding to Lydia Hayes: a certain tract or parcel of land in Wake Forest, Wake County, State of North Carolina, adjoining the lands of Dennie Kearney, Will Debnam and others: BEGINNING at a stake on the North Side of Fort Street, Dennis Kearney's corner and running along said street in an Easterly direction sixty (60) feet; thence in a Northerly direction along the line of land of J. R. Mangum one hundred twenty (120) feet and three (3) inches; thence in a Westerly direction along the line of land of Will Debnam sixty (60) feet to a stake Dennis Kearney's line; thence in a Southerly direction along the line of land of Dennis Kearney one hundred twenty (120) feet and three (3) inches to the point of BEGINNING, Together with the right to the use of a well situated between the above described lot and a lot described as belonging to J. R. Mangum.
document_extractions.legal_description_short: Three tracts in Buckhorn Township, Wake County, NC (25.17 ac, 14.77 ac, 23.07 ac) conveyed to D. A. Baker and wife Annie B. Baker; plus a 60x120 ft lot on North Side of Fort Street, Wake Forest, Wake County, NC conveyed to Lydia Hayes.
document_extractions.references_prior_deed_language: being the same land which was conveyed to Rudolph J. Baker by J. E. Pearson, Commissioner by deed dated October 11, 1930 and recorded in Book 608 at page 217, Wake County Registry. See Book 857 page 449, Wake County Registry; being the same identical land described in a deed dated April 22, 1919, by Clarence J. Smith, Trustee, to J. A. Hockaday, and recorded in Book 338 at page 597, in Wake County Registry. This is the same land that was conveyed to Rudolph J. Baker by J. A. Hockaday and wife Florence Hockaday by deed dated August 20, 1930, and recorded in Book 592 page 426, Wake County Registry. See also Book 857 page 449, Wake County Registry; being the same land which was conveyed to Rudolph J. Baker by Wm. B. Oliver and wife, see Book 608 page 220, Wake County Registry.
document_extractions.legal_match_notes: No parcel ID, subdivision, lot, plat book, or plat page was provided in the parcel_reference to match against. The document contains two separate deeds recorded consecutively at Book 913 beginning at page 352, conveying four distinct parcels across two transactions. Without additional parcel reference data, a specific match cannot be confirmed.
document_extractions.summary: This record contains two warranty deeds recorded consecutively in Book 913 at page 352, Wake County, NC: (1) Kathryn Baker Cummings and husband Paul Cummings conveying three rural tracts in Buckhorn Township (totaling approximately 63 acres) to D. A. Baker and wife Annie B. Baker, dated September 29, 1944; and (2) W. W. Holding and wife Josephine H. Holding conveying a 60x120 ft lot on Fort Street in Wake Forest to Lydia Hayes, dated August 1, 1944, registered November 6, 1944.

document_extractions.legal_description_full: BEGINS AT A STONE ON WEST SIDE OF ROAD FROM W. O. ALLEN'S PLACE TO CHURCH STREET IN THE TOWN OF WAKE FOREST AND RUNS ALONG THE WESTERN BOUNDRY OF SAID ROAD N. 1/2 DEGREE E. 2 11/100 CHAINS TO A STONE WILL DEBNAM'S SOUTH EAST CORNER; THENCE WITH HIS LINE N. 65 DEGREES W. 1 73/100 CHAINS TO A STONE CHAMPION KEARNEY'S CORNER; THENCE WITH HIS LINE S. 15 DEGREES WEST 2 CHAINS TO A STONE ON NORTH SIDE OF EAST PINE STREET OF SAID TOWN; THENCE WITH THE NORTHERN BOUNDARY OF SAID STREET S. 65 DEGREES E. 2 19/100 CHAINS TO THE BEGINNING. BEING LOTS 19 AND 19A OF LANDS CONVEYED TO MOSES FORT BY MRS. M. E. SIMMONS, H. A. CHAPPELL, SURVEYOR, RECORDED IN OFFICE OF REGISTER OF DEEDS FOR WAKE COUNTY, IN BOOK 232, AT PAGE 297 AND BOOK 657, AT PAGE 368, AND BOOK 856 AT PAGE 598.
